title_en: Morello Cherry Ratafia (Variant I, continued from previous page)

ingredients.note: 60° strength, used for initial one-day maceration

steps_en: Let 20 liters macerate for one day with 60° alcohol.
To the distillate add: rose water, cherry water, raspberry water, orange blossom water (0.5 L each) and syrup (0.5 L).
notes_en: This passage appears to be the continuation of a recipe begun on the previous page; the opening ingredient list is missing.

title_en: Morello Cherry and Raspberry Liqueur (Method II)

ingredients.note: per liter of perfumed alcohol, for the syrup added cold
used to top up the final volume to 10 liters

steps_en: Crush the fruit and let it ferment.
Before distillation, add 750 g of peach kernels and 500 g of orris root.
Distill slowly to recover all the alcohol.
Add cold a syrup made with 1.850 kg of sugar per liter of the perfumed alcohol.
Bring the total volume to 10 liters using 3.50 liters of alcohol.

title_en: Morello Cherry and Raspberry Liqueur (Method III)

ingredients.name_en: Leaves of the same plant (cherry/raspberry leaves)

ingredients.note: added at the end once the liqueur has cooled

steps_en: Ferment 180 parts of sour/morello cherries with 10-30 parts of raspberries and 10-12 parts of leaves of the same plant.
When fermentation has reached the proper stage, distill the liquid together with a little peach kernel and 1 part of crushed Florentine iris root.
Alternatively, macerate the crushed fruit and other ingredients for two or three days with 160 to 200 parts of wine spirit, then distill in a bain-marie still to recover all the spirit used.
If the liqueur is not strong enough, rectify it again on fresh material; this yields a liqueur with a more delicate and more fragrant aroma.
When rectifying, a little walnut and a little iris root may be added to the still.
After a few hours, once the liqueur has cooled, add the syrup.
notes_en: Proportions given in relative 'parts' (p.) rather than absolute weights; ranges in the original text are captured as qty (low) plus a variant (high).

title_en: Morello Cherry Liqueur (Method IV) — recipe continues on next page
steps_en: Remove the stem and the pit from 12 kilograms of morello cherries.
Place the pitted cherries in fresh water and let them soak for three days.
Then take the pits, place them in a still... (recipe continues on the following page).
notes_en: The recipe is cut off at the bottom of the page and continues on the next page.
